lesson.title: Sprint back. Stop the ball.
lesson.body_md:
## Transition is where games are lost

The other team just got the ball. They're sprinting the other way. The next 4 seconds decide if they get a layup or a tough shot.

```takeaway
Most players jog back. You sprint. That's the whole game.
```

## Two simple jobs

### Job 1 — Stop the ball
The **first** player back doesn't run to their guy. They run to the **basketball**. Get in front of it. Make the dribbler slow down.

```tip
Don't reach. Don't gamble. Just be a wall.
```

### Job 2 — Find shooters
The second and third players back run to the **wings and corners**. The rim is already covered.

```reveal
Q: Why don't you sprint to the rim?
A: Because everyone else is. The shots that kill you in transition are corner threes and trailing threes — not layups.
```

## Test yourself

```quiz
Q: Their fast break is coming. You're the first player back. Where do you go?
- To your assigned guy ✗
- To the ball ✓
- To the rim ✗
Why: First back stops the ball. Buys your teammates time to get matched up.
```

```quiz
Q: You're the third player back. The ball is stopped. What do you do?
- Sprint to your original guy across the floor ✗
- Find the closest open shooter ✓
- Run to the rim ✗
Why: Match up to the closest threat. Sort out original assignments at the next dead ball.
```

## When to take a charge

Feet planted, outside the restricted arc, ball handler bearing down? Take it.

```mistake
Moving sideways when they hit you. That's a block. Plant your feet first.
```

## Watch out for

```mistake
Watching the shot go up. By the time you turn, the offense has a layup.
Fix: when the shot leaves their hand, you turn and sprint.
```

```mistake
Backpedaling. You give up a step every move.
Fix: turn and run. Square up only when you're at the level of the ball.
```

## Talk loud

- **"Back!"** — when their shot goes up
- **"I got ball!"** — first player back
- **"I got trailer!"** — pickup on a late shooter

```takeaway
Sprint. Sort. Swarm. Stop the ball first. Find shooters second. Match up last.
```

Now try it in practice.
